assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_067",
  "rule_id": "Ensure Chrony is only configured with the server directive",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.92,
  "rationale": "The finding concerns restricting Chrony configuration to server directives, which is a standard time synchronization hardening step. Remediation involves editing a configuration file and does not affect authentication, network stack, or filesystem layout, making it low risk and reversible.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "potential loss of accurate time synchronization if misconfigured"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Create a backup of /etc/chrony.conf",
    "Edit the file to ensure only server lines are present and remove any peer or allow directives",
    "Validate the configuration with 'chronyc sources'",
    "Restart the chronyd service using systemctl restart chronyd",
    "Verify time sync status with 'chronyc tracking'"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
